channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: One Dawlah Islamiya member killed and two captured in Datu Hoffer, Maguindanao del Sur
item.description: In Datu Hoffer, Maguindanao del Sur, Philippine Army soldiers engaged with remaining members of the now-defunct Dawlah Islamiya group on Saturday, May 30. The encounter resulted in the death of Andami...

item.title: Marilyn Monroe and Her Cats: A Glimpse into the Star’s Love for Animals
item.description: Marilyn Monroe, one of Hollywood’s most iconic figures, was also known for her deep affection for animals, particularly cats. Throughout her life, she owned several feline companions, finding comfort ...

item.title: Trump criticizes political opponents as Iran war negotiations stall
item.description: President Donald Trump posted a series of early-morning messages on his Truth Social platform criticizing both Democrats and members of his own party as he struggles to negotiate an end to the ongoing...

item.title: Opinion: Criticism of Steven Guilbeault’s environmental legacy and federal climate policy approach
item.description: This opinion piece by Rob Breakenridge critiques former federal environment minister Steven Guilbeault and his role in shaping Canada’s climate and energy policies during the Trudeau era. The author a...

item.title: Senator Jinggoy Estrada surrenders to CIDG following Sandiganbayan arrest order
item.description: Senator Jinggoy Estrada voluntarily surrendered to the Criminal Investigation and Detection Group (CIDG) at Camp Crame on Monday, June 1, after the Sandiganbayan issued an arrest order in connection w...

item.title: Israeli Attorney General Raises Alarm Over Erosion of Judicial Independence Under Netanyahu Government
item.description: Israel’s Attorney General Gali Baharav-Miara has expressed serious concerns about what she described as growing democratic backsliding in the country under Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s governme...

item.title: NWU research explores cancer-fighting potential of indigenous cancer bush plant
item.description: Researchers at North-West University (NWU) in South Africa are investigating the potential of an indigenous medicinal plant, Lessertia frutescens—commonly known as cancer bush—as a possible source for...

item.title: Bafana Bafana to depart for World Cup on Monday after visa issues resolved
item.description: The South African Football Association (Safa) has confirmed that the majority of Bafana Bafana players and technical staff have received their US visas, and the team’s plane is scheduled to depart for...

item.title: DOLE tightens workplace safety enforcement following Angeles City building collapse
item.description: The Department of Labor and Employment (DOLE) has renewed its focus on enforcing occupational health and safety (OHS) standards following the collapse of a building in Angeles City. The incident has r...

item.title: Bilawal stresses protection of Gilgit-Baltistan’s rights ahead of elections
item.description: PPP Chairperson Bilawal Bhutto-Zardari, while addressing a rally in Shigar, Gilgit-Baltistan, emphasised that any future constitutional amendment must protect the rights of the people of Gilgit-Baltis...

item.title: Cybersecurity flaws and exam irregularities shake India’s national entrance and school assessment systems
item.description: India’s education system has come under intense scrutiny following the emergence of cybersecurity vulnerabilities in a key online marking platform and wider irregularities affecting national examinati...

item.title: Limpopo School Struggles To Feed Learners During Rainy Weather
item.description: Learners at Boxahuku Primary in Qaza village, Vhembe East, Limpopo, often miss out on meals during rainy days. This no-fee school relies on a feeding scheme to provide food to around 600 pupils, many ...

item.title: Access barriers to assisted dying in Manitoba highlighted by 94-year-old patient’s case
item.description: A 94-year-old woman from Steinbach, Manitoba, is seeking medical assistance in dying (MAID) but has been unable to access it due to legal and systemic barriers, according to her family and advocacy gr...

item.title: Africa CDC acknowledges India’s emergency medical assistance amid Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo
item.description: The Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention (Africa CDC) has expressed gratitude to India for providing urgent medical assistance to support efforts against the ongoing Ebola outbreak in the...

item.title: IFAB approves rule changes for 2026 World Cup addressing conduct, time-wasting, and VAR updates
item.description: The International Football Association Board (IFAB) has approved a series of significant rule changes that will take effect starting with the 2026–27 football season and will also be implemented at th...

item.title: Keir Starmer outlines housing reform agenda focused on expanding social housing and tackling shortages
item.description: In this opinion piece, UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer sets out his vision for addressing the housing crisis through what he describes as a “politics of home”. He reflects on the importance of stable h...

item.title: Former Canadian Forces major urges Ottawa to honour Afghan resettlement promise for at-risk interpreter
item.description: A retired Canadian Forces major is urging the federal government to honour its commitments to Afghan nationals who supported Canadian and NATO missions, highlighting the case of an Afghan man currentl...

item.title: Investigation finds congressional press credential system granted access to ineligible individuals, including convicted offender and political operatives
item.description: A Raw Story investigation reports serious irregularities in how congressional press credentials are issued by the Radio-Television Correspondents’ Association (RTCA), a powerful but little-known body ...

item.title: Canada Post workers await decision on proposed tentative labour agreement
item.description: Canada Post workers are expected to learn whether a new tentative labour agreement has been reached, according to reporting from The Canadian Press published on June 1, 2026. The development comes ami...

item.title: Former First Minister Urges Joint Inquiry into Peter Murrell's Embezzlement
item.description: Former Labour First Minister Jack McConnell has called for a joint inquiry by the UK and Scottish parliaments into financial crimes committed by Peter Murrell, ex-SNP chief executive and estranged hus...
